title: eriogonum vimineum subsp. virgatum
intro: Eriogonum vimineum subsp. virgatum, also known as the winecup or desert winecup, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is a member of the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae) and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Eriogonum vimineum subsp. virgatum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for various health conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, or used in salves and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
